entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; hosts job board and talent analysis platform for candidates and employers

entities.name: Alumni
entities.context: VC firm mentioned as a target client for Careerspan's VC-facing offering

entities.name: Alex
entities.context: Collaborator or advisor who frames the value proposition as a 'smoothing function'

entities.name: Smoothing Function
entities.context: Alex's framing — turning lumpy company-by-company hiring into steady portfolio-wide talent flow

entities.name: VC-Facing Offering
entities.context: Proposed Careerspan product sitting on top of existing jobs boards to capture and route talent to portcos

entities.name: Careerspan Job Board
entities.context: Simple, Careerspan-hosted job board candidates revisit; provides richer per-role analysis upon application

entities.name: Structured Talent Pools
entities.context: Organized candidate pools built from job board visitors, routed to relevant portfolio companies

entities.name: High-Signal Candidates
entities.context: 2–5% of truly high-fit people per role surfaced by Careerspan's analysis

entities.name: Quality Filtering Incentive
entities.context: Aligned incentive: it costs Careerspan more to surface unqualified people, so the product inherently filters for quality

entities.name: Evergreen Pipeline
entities.context: Ongoing pipeline of analyzed candidates that can be evaluated against multiple portco needs

relationships.context: Careerspan proposes a VC-facing offering targeting VCs like Alumni
Alex frames Careerspan's VC value prop as a smoothing function for portfolio-wide hiring
Alex contributes strategic framing for Careerspan's VC-facing pitch
Careerspan hosts and operates the job board as the candidate entry point
The VC offering sits on top of the existing jobs board as a call to action
Careerspan captures job board visitors into structured talent pools for portco routing
Talent pools are analyzed to surface 2–5% high-fit candidates per role
Careerspan's cost structure inherently motivates filtering for candidate quality
Careerspan builds an evergreen pipeline as candidates share analyzable stories over time
The smoothing function concept describes the evergreen pipeline's effect on portfolio hiring
